topic: Artistic Culture in the German-speaking World
subject: german

items.q: Which famous composer of German-speaking culture was born in Bonn?
items.why: Students confuse European composers; Beethoven, born in Bonn, is a central figure of German artistic heritage.

items.q: The Berlinale is an example of which cultural event?
items.why: Students don't recognise major cultural institutions; the Berlinale celebrates film, part of artistic culture.

items.q: Is German literature limited to writers from Germany?
items.correct: No — it includes Austrian and Swiss authors writing in German
items.misconception: Yes, only writers born in Germany
items.random_wrong: Only authors who write in English
items.why: Students equate it with one country; the German-speaking literary world spans Germany, Austria and Switzerland.

items.q: Which movement in the visual arts and design originated in Germany?
items.why: Correct answer: The Bauhaus. “Impressionism” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Goethe and Kafka are best known as figures in which art form?
items.why: Correct answer: Literature. “Painting” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
